psinematics.spellerror.nullconstruct: ERROR: Spell Construct target is non existent.
psinematics.spellerror.assembly_already_: ERROR: Assembly is already in progress.
psinematics.spellerror.point_out_of_sub_: ERROR: Target point is outside of target construct.
psinematics.spellerror.point_inside_sub_: ERROR: Target point is already inside of a construct.
psinematics.spellerror.too_many_blocks: ERROR: Too many blocks were selected.
psinematics.spellerror.imbalanced_begin_: Begin Assembly requires later End Assembly trick
End Assembly requires earlier Begin Assembly trick
psinematics.spellpiece.current_sub_level: Returns the construct that the caster is inside of
Returns the construct last assembled by this spell
psinematics.spellpiece.containing_sub_le: Returns the construct that the target point is inside of
psinematics.spellpiece.locate_center_of_: Locates the center of mass of the target construct
psinematics.spellpiece.begin_assembly.de: Begins creation of a new construct (via blockworks spells)
psinematics.spellpiece.end_assembly.desc: Completes a constructed started by Begin Assembly
psinematics.spellpiece.assemble_connecte: Combines connected blocks into a construct
psinematics.spellpiece.disassemble.desc: Disassembles a construct into ordinary blocks
psinematics.spellpiece.add_angular_momen: Applies angular momentum to a construct
psinematics.book.entry.tutorial.name: Kinematics
psinematics.book.entry.tutorial.desc: Spells can interact with blocks that have been given physics, referred to as Constructs.
psinematics.book.entry.tutorial.assemble: The simplest way to create a construct is by assembling contiguous blocks into one. This trick can only assemble a limited number of blocks, which the cost and potency scales with; if too many blocks are connected, the trick fails entirely.
psinematics.book.entry.tutorial.disassem: Conversely, constructs can be disassembled, turning them back into ordinary blocks. This also has a block limit; if you need to recover a large structure, consider breaking it into pieces first.
psinematics.book.entry.tutorial.current_: A spell that creates a construct can get ahold of the construct it just created. This selector must evaluate after a trick was executed to create one, and will evaluate to null otherwise.
psinematics.book.entry.tutorial.begin_en: All ordinary block placement spells can be used to create constructs too, using $(piece)Trick: Begin Assembly$(0). Once executed, further block placement spells (such as $(piece)Trick: Place Block$(0) or $(piece)Trick: Conjure Block Sequence$(0)) will place them in the form of a new construct, aligned to but decoupled from the world grid. All placed blocks will be bound together in a single construct.$(p)To return to placing ordinary blocks, use $(piece)Trick: End Assembly$(0). This also allows creating multiple constructs (by alternating Begin/End), and allows $(piece)Trick: Completed Assembly$(0) to reference the newly created construct. The example spell on the right demonstrates this.
psinematics.book.entry.tutorial.containi: Spells may look for constructs anywhere in the world...
psinematics.book.entry.tutorial.current_: ...or ask for the caster's vessel. Unlike $(piece)Selector: Containing Construct$(0), this finds the construct the caster is riding on, even if they're only stood on top, or riding a creature standing on it, etc.
psinematics.book.entry.tutorial.locate_c: Most spells manipulating constructs will prefer to do so at their center of mass, to keep it stable. This is straightforward in a spell, though costs additional complexity for the convenience.
psinematics.book.entry.tutorial.add_mome: Unlike when manipulating entities, spells apply momentum to constructs rather than raw motion. Heavier constructs will be affected less.
